data.title: Brazilian steelmaker CSN pushes ahead with cement unit sale
data.description:
By Luciana Magalhaes

Brazilian steelmaker CSN (CSNA3) is pushing ahead with the sale of its cement unit, which could fetch over 10 billion reais ($2 billion), according to two people familiar with the matter.

The talks involve local and international players, including Brazil's Votorantim and J&F S.A., which also controls meatpacker JBS, as well as Chinese companies Anhui Conch Cement, Huaxin Cement, which acquired a Brazilian company in 2024, and Sinoma International.

The interest from these Chinese groups was reported earlier in the day by the Brazilian newspaper Valor Econômico and independently confirmed by Reuters.

CSN, Votorantim, and J&F declined to comment. Anhui Conch, Huaxin, and Sinoma did not respond to requests for comment.

Votorantim could bid on the cement maker solo or with a partner if it decides to pursue the acquisition, according to one of the sources. The same person, who spoke on condition of anonymity, citing the confidential nature of the talks, said J&F was discussing a potential offer of 10 billion reais for the asset.

As part of its efforts to reduce debt, CSN is divesting certain assets. Chief Financial Officer Marco Rabello recently said that the company expects to complete the sale of its cement unit, CSN Cimentos, and sell a stake in its logistics company by the third quarter, potentially raising up to 18 billion reais.

Rabello also said that CSN had hired Morgan Stanley to advise on the sale of control of CSN Cimentos and mandated Bradesco and Citibank to advise on the process involving its logistics company.

data.title: Digital Brand Media & Marketing Group posts $85.7K revenue, $(0.00) EPS in 10-Q
data.description:
Digital Brand Media & Marketing Group reported six-month revenue of $85,689 and a net loss per share of $(0.00) as disclosed in its Form 10-Q; revenue rose 51% year-over-year while net loss widened to $(550,206) due to other expenses. Operating loss improved to $(269,287) from the prior-year period despite the net loss deterioration. The company highlighted progress on its DCIE prototype, pilot deployments and a strategic shift toward AI-augmented consulting and subscription offerings.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues: $0.0857 million (six months ended Feb 28, 2026: $85,689), up 51% vs prior year.
  • Gross Profit: $0.0303 million (six months; Revenues $85,689 less Cost of revenues $55,405 = $30,284).
  • Operating Loss: $(0.2693) million (six months ended Feb 28, 2026: $(269,287)), improved vs prior year loss of $(320,372).
  • Net Income (Loss): $(0.5502) million (six months ended Feb 28, 2026: $(550,206)), deterioration vs prior year $(170,510) driven by other expenses.
  • Net Loss Per Share: $(0.00) basic and diluted (six months ended Feb 28, 2026; weighted average shares 865,218,631).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Growth: Revenues rose 51% year-over-year for the six months ended Feb 28, 2026, reflecting increased demand for enhanced services and pilot conversions.
  • Channel & Service Shift: The company exited commoditized tactical services to focus on higher-value AI-augmented go-to-market (GTM) consulting and subscription platform offerings.
  • Brand Momentum: Increased investor and market visibility through OTC conference participation, thought leadership, and expanded digital presence.
  • Operational Milestones: Completed DCIE prototype, advanced to pilot deployment and commercial launch in Q1 FY2026 with subscription and white-label paths.
  • Go-to-Market Expansion: Prioritizing U.S. footprint for 2026–2027 and launching a new investor-facing GTM Audit service targeting private equity, venture capital and their portfolio companies.

Original SEC Filing: Digital Brand Media & Marketing Group, Inc. [ DBMM ] - 10-Q - Apr. 14, 2026

data.title: Why Is GTLB Stock Up 7% After Hours Today?
data.description:
Shares of GitLab Inc. (GTLB) jumped 7% in after-hours trading Tuesday following the company’s announcement of a deepened collaboration with Google Cloud that integrates Vertex AI models directly into GitLab’s Duo Agent Platform.

GitLab closed the regular session at $20.08. In extended trading, the stock climbed as high as $21.62, before tempering some gains. 

The partnership lets enterprise teams power GitLab’s AI agents with Google Cloud’s Vertex AI foundation models — including Gemini — while keeping all agent actions under GitLab’s existing compliance, audit, and governance controls.

Developers no longer need to manage separate AI infrastructure: GitLab’s AI Gateway can run natively on Google Cloud runtimes such as GKE or Cloud Run. Customers with Google Cloud spending commitments can also apply their GitLab Duo usage toward those budgets.

Get updates to this developing story directly on Stocktwits.

data.title: U.S. Says Its Blockade Is Working. How Some Ships Are Still Getting Through the Strait. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Anita Hamilton

The U.S. Naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz appears to be effective so far, according to U.S. Central Command. In a Tuesday post on X, it wrote that "no ships from Iranian ports made it past the blockade" enforced by warships there since Monday.

However, commercial ships that aren't stopping at Iranian ports appear to be making it through. "More than 20 commercial ships have passed through the Strait of Hormuz in the past 24 hours," The Wall Street Journal reported. The White House did not immediately reply to a request for comment.

Yesterday, just six ships made it through the strait, down from 14 on Saturday and another 14 on Sunday, according to ship-tracking service Kpler. The U.S. blockade is "pushing many shipowners and operators into a wait-and-see posture," it added. Kpler hasn't provided data on Tuesday's crossings yet.

Iran agreed to open the strait as part of the cease-fire deal it struck with the U.S. a week ago. But reports of tolls as high as $2 million per vessel combined with safety concerns revolving around possible attacks or setting off sea mines that may still be planted there have brought traffic to a virtual standstill. Iran previously said that the strait was closed to ships from countries that support the U.S. or Israel.

Geographic constraints within the strait also make passage extremely risky. Because of shallow areas in the waterway, ships can only pass through two lanes, each of which is about two miles wide. "This leaves vessels extremely vulnerable to missile and small-boat attacks," The Washington Post wrote.

Write to Anita Hamilton at anita.hamilton@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Ralliant Announces First Quarter 2026 Earnings Release and Conference Call Date
data.description:
RALEIGH, N.C.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--April 14, 2026--

Ralliant Corporation ("Ralliant" or the "Company") will release earnings for the first quarter of 2026 before the opening of the New York Stock Exchange on Tuesday, May 12, 2026. The Company will host a conference call on Tuesday, May 12, 2026, at 8:30 a.m. ET, to discuss the results.

The conference call can be accessed by dialing 877-407-8211 within the U.S. or +1 201-389-0902 outside the U.S. before 8:30 a.m. ET and notifying the operator that you are dialing in for Ralliant's earnings conference call. Access to the real-time audio webcast may be found on the Ralliant Investor Relations website at https://investors.ralliant.com, where related materials will be posted prior to the conference call and a replay of the webcast will be available for six months following the conference call.

About Ralliant

Ralliant is a global provider of precision technologies that specializes in designing, developing, manufacturing, and servicing precision instruments and highly engineered products. Ralliant's two strategic reporting segments — Sensors & Safety Systems and Test & Measurement — include well-known brands with leading positions in their markets. The Company's businesses empower engineers with precision technologies essential for breakthrough innovation that brings advanced technologies to the market faster and more efficiently. With over 150 years of operating experience and enduring customer trust, we are known for delivering innovative, high-quality products with the precision that mission-critical systems demand. Ralliant is headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina and employs a team of approximately 7,000 research and development, manufacturing, sales, distribution, service, and administrative employees. The Company's global footprint enables a unique 'engineer to engineer' approach, which allows it to build enduring trust, credibility, and partnerships with customers across both Fortune 1000 companies and next generation start-up enterprises. With a culture rooted in continuous improvement, the core of the Company's operating model is the Ralliant Business System. For more information please visit: www.ralliant.com.

data.title: FNB Corp. Authorizes $250M Share Buyback Program, Boosts Dividend
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

FNB Corp. authorized a $250 million share repurchase program and raised its quarterly dividend by 8%.

The financial services company said the new share buyback adds to the remaining $50 million left under its prior repurchase program authorized in April 2022.

The company also declared a quarterly dividend of 13 cents a share, up from 12 cents a share.

The new payout, equal to 52 cents a year, represents an annual yield of about 2.9% based on Tuesday's closing price of $17.73.

The dividend is payable on June 15 to stockholders of record on June 1, the company said.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Quaker Chemical Refinances With $1.6 Billion Secured Credit Facilities Led by Bank of America
data.description:
Quaker Chemical entered into an amended credit agreement to establish new senior secured facilities totaling approximately $1.6 billion to refinance its existing credit agreement and fund general corporate purposes. The package includes a euro-denominated term loan equivalent to $250 million, a $550 million U.S. dollar term loan, and an $800 million revolving credit facility, each subject to customary covenants and leverage tests. Borrowings bear interest at Base Rate or Term SOFR for U.S. dollars, or Alternative Currency Term Rates for other currencies, with margins tied to leverage. The facilities mature on April 10, 2031, enhancing the company’s liquidity and flexibility.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Senior secured euro and U.S. dollar term loans and revolving credit facility
  • Counterparty: Bank of America, as Administrative Agent, and other lenders
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 10 2026 / Apr 10 2026
  • Duration / Termination: 5 years
  • Reason: Refinance existing debt and enhance liquidity

Original SEC Filing: QUAKER CHEMICAL CORP [ KWR ] - 8-K - Apr. 14, 2026

Disclaimer

Alcoa announced it will redeem all $218.742 million of its subsidiary's 6.125% notes due 2028 on May 15, 2026.

Key Highlights:

  • Wholly-owned subsidiary Alcoa Nederland Holding B.V. elected to redeem all outstanding 6.125% notes due 2028.
  • Aggregate outstanding principal as of April 14, 2026: $218,742,000 (reported as $219 million in press release).
  • Redemption date set for May 15, 2026, at 100.000% of principal plus accrued interest to but not including the redemption date.
  • Notes are senior unsecured and guaranteed by Alcoa and certain subsidiaries under the Indenture.
  • Redemption will be funded using cash on hand, per the company's press release.

data.title: He Championed the Trumps' Crypto Venture. Now He's Attacking It. — WSJ
data.description:
By Sam Kessler and Vicky Ge Huang

Crypto entrepreneur Justin Sun once championed the Trump family's flagship venture into digital assets. Now he's accusing the outfit of treating him and other backers as its "personal ATM."

Sun accused World Liberty Financial of blocking him from selling a stake he first amassed in the company in late 2024, just after President Trump was elected to a second term. At the time, the Chinese-born billionaire was facing Securities and Exchange Commission charges of fraud and market manipulation. Last month, Sun settled his case with the SEC without admitting wrongdoing.

Sun's beef began in September, when World Liberty unlocked 20% of its digital tokens, allowing investors to trade some of their holdings on the open market for the first time. The move notched $5 billion in paper gains for the Trump family, but Sun took to the social-media platform X to argue his WLFI tokens had been "unreasonably frozen." At the time, World Liberty didn't directly address Sun's allegations but said in a statement on X that it responds to "malicious or high-risk activity that could harm community members."

Sun, a vocal figure within the crypto industry who has courted publicity for his staunch support of Trump, stepped up his accusations against World Liberty earlier this month, after the crypto company pledged its own WLFI tokens as collateral to borrow more than $75 million in stablecoins from Dolomite, a lending platform founded by World Liberty's chief technology officer. The move, which was earlier reported by CoinDesk, left some users who had deposited stablecoins in Dolomite unable to fully withdraw their funds.

Sun seized on the Dolomite outrage — and discontent from individual investors who remain locked out of 80% of their tokens — to voice his own frustrations with World Liberty, accusing it of using a secret "backdoor blacklisting function" to freeze his tokens. The company fired back from its official X account on April 12: "Justin's favorite move is playing the victim while making baseless allegations to cover up his own misconduct."

Sun and World Liberty had held talks to resolve their dispute but haven't reached an agreement, according to people familiar with the matter.

"See you in court pal," World Liberty said in its X post.

Sun's path from World Liberty's savior to an unlikely leader of an investor rebellion began less than two years ago, when his $30 million investment saved the startup from a disappointing fundraising.

Trump and his sons had pitched World Liberty as a radical re-envisioning of the financial system. In October 2024, the company began selling a token, WLFI, which it said would offer holders a way to vote on the company's future.

The token offering came with an unusual constraint: It could be purchased but not resold. World Liberty set an ambitious $300 million fundraising target, but slashed it by 90%, to $30 million, after two weeks of lackluster sales.

Sun's investment catapulted World Liberty past its goal in a single transaction. Sun was named an official adviser to World Liberty and helped promote the company alongside its founders.

His endorsement sent money flooding in from other investors, propelling World Liberty to $550 million in total sales by the conclusion of its fundraising. Sun ultimately purchased at least $75 million in tokens.

Under the terms disclosed on World Liberty's website, 75% of revenue from token sales flowed to entities affiliated with the Trump family. Members of Congress criticized the arrangement as a backdoor channel for funneling money to the incoming president.

They pointed to Sun as the most visible illustration of this dynamic: In addition to investing in World Liberty, Sun has been the biggest public investor in $TRUMP, the president's memecoin, and attended a May 2025 dinner at Trump's golf club with other big investors.

Sun's matter with the SEC was settled in March: One of his companies agreed to pay a $10 million civil penalty, and the remaining charges against him were dismissed with prejudice.

While Sun remained World Liberty's best-known investor throughout most of 2025, his profile within the company had fallen behind the scenes in favor of newer, deeper-pocketed allies.

The Wall Street Journal reported that in January 2025, World Liberty had secretly taken in a $500 million investment from a group led by Sheikh Tahnoon bin Zayed Al Nahyan, a member of the United Arab Emirates' royal family. The new investors acquired a 49% stake in the corporate entity behind World Liberty, according to company documents reviewed by the Journal.

World Liberty also deepened its relationship with Changpeng Zhao, the billionaire founder of Binance, the world's largest crypto exchange. Binance listed WLFI and played a central role in promoting World Liberty's stablecoin, USD1.

When Sun's tokens were frozen following the 20% unlocking in September, traders speculated that the move came because he had attempted to unwind his large holdings, threatening to tank WLFI's price. Sun said on X that allegations he'd tried to sell his tokens were false.

The freeze on Sun's 544 million WLFI tokens has remained in place ever since, according to blockchain research firm Bubblemaps.

Retail investors, meanwhile, have voiced frustration that they, too, have been locked out of selling 80% of their tokens.

Of particular frustration to these traders: World Liberty has found creative ways to monetize its WLFI holdings while blocking retail traders from selling theirs. In August 2025, for instance, World Liberty transferred billions of WLFI tokens to ALT5 Sigma, a publicly traded company, as part of a $1.5 billion deal that netted the project equity in the firm, according to securities filings.

"It would be false to suggest that World Liberty is 'exiting' any positions," said David Wachsman, a spokesperson for World Liberty Financial. "We are incredibly confident in what we are building and in the value of the WLFI governance token. That's why we've already bought back more than $65 million in WLFI tokens and have been so deliberate in curating proposals."

When the news broke in April that World Liberty had used its WLFI tokens as collateral to borrow stablecoins on Dolomite, investors grew even more outraged.

"The whole taking a loan on your own token as collateral is tremendously shady," said Morten Christensen, the founder of airdropalert.com and a WLFI investor. "I'm glad to see that the community is pushing back hard on that."

Of particular concern to investors like Christensen: If World Liberty is liquidated, a massive number of WLFI tokens would flood the market, causing the token's price to drop even further before individual investors can exit their positions.

"We are nowhere near liquidation — and frankly, even if markets moved dramatically against us, we'd simply supply more collateral," World Liberty said in an X post.

In response to the backlash, the company announced on X that it would host a governance vote to unlock the remaining tokens. In a follow-up post, it clarified that it wouldn't release all of the tokens at once.

Write to Sam Kessler at sam.kessler@wsj.com and Vicky Ge Huang at vicky.huang@wsj.com

data.title: TSX rises to six-week high on Mideast optimism
data.description:
Key points:
  • TSX ends up 0.7% at 34,102.36
  • Posts highest closing level since March 2
  • Tech adds 2.8% with Keel Infrastructure up 21.4%
  • Materials group gains 1.2% as gold rises

By Fergal Smith

Canada's main stock index rose on Tuesday, led by technology and metal mining shares, as a drop in oil prices helped investors look past recent geopolitical uncertainty.

The Toronto Stock Exchange's S&P/TSX Composite Index ended up 223.12 points, or 0.7%, at 34,102.36, marking the highest closing level since March 2, when the index posted a record high.

"This is the definition of 'climbing the wall of worry,'" said Greg Taylor, chief investment officer at PenderFund Capital Management, referring to the ability of stocks to rise despite challenges to the outlook.

"There's so much fear out there, and it seems like the market is shrugging it off. We had a big reversal in oil prices, and that's gotten a lot of people coming back to risk assets," Taylor said.

The price of oil settled 7.9% lower at $91.28 a barrel on hopes that Iran will resume talks with the United States to end the conflict, which has shut the Strait of Hormuz.

Talks to end the Iran war could resume in Pakistan over the next two days, U.S. President Donald Trump said.

In Canada, Prime Minister Mark Carney announced a temporary suspension of a fuel tax one day after his Liberal Party swept three special elections to secure a parliamentary majority.

"We've got some negativity on the energy stocks but that's being offset by some strength in these other sectors," Taylor said.

Technology added 2.8%, with shares of data center company Keel Infrastructure Corp up 21.4%.

The materials group , which includes metal mining shares, ended 1.2% higher. It was helped by a 2.2% advance in the price of gold as reduced safe-haven demand and encouraging U.S. producer price data weighed on the U.S. dollar.

Heavily weighted financials (.SPTTFS) gained 1.2% as investors assessed U.S. bank earnings.

Shares of MDA Space rose 7.1% to the highest level since August 2025 after Amazon.com said it will acquire Globalstar . MDA is the primary contractor for Globalstar's next-generation constellation.

Energy was the biggest decliner, falling 2.4%.

data.title: Invesco Mortgage Capital Appoints President as CEO
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Invesco Mortgage Capital named President Kevin Collins as its next chief executive officer, effective May 1.

Collins will succeed John Anzalone, who is retiring from the company effective April 30 after nine years as CEO, the real estate investment trust said Tuesday. Anzalone will remain as an adviser to the company through Oct. 1.

Collins has been Invesco Mortgage Capital's president since 2017, and has worked in several capacities with the company dating back to its formation in 2009, the company said. He is a co-head of structured investments for Invesco Fixed Income.

David Lyle will succeed Collins as president, effective May 1. Lyle has been Invesco Mortgage Capital's chief operating officer since 2017 and has been involved in the management of the company since 2009. He is also the co-head of structured investments for Invesco Fixed Income.

Invesco Mortgage Capital is managed by a subsidiary of investment management firm Invesco.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Equity Bancshares Q1 net interest income surges
data.description:
Overview

  • U.S. regional bank's Q1 net interest income rose 16% qtr/qtr and 46.5% yoy

  • Company completed Frontier Holdings acquisition on Jan. 1, 2026, adding $1.3 bln in loans and $1.1 bln in deposits

Outlook

  • Company continues to estimate allowance for credit losses with assumptions of slower prepayment rates and market disruption

Result Drivers

  • FRONTIER ACQUISITION - Integration of Frontier Holdings drove growth in loans and deposits

  • MARGIN IMPACT - Addition of Frontier's assets and liabilities diluted net interest margin, partially offset by higher-than-expected loan accretion

  • HIGHER CREDIT PROVISION - Provision for credit losses of $6 mln due to integration of Frontier balances into reserve framework

data.title: Infinity Natural Rscs Files 8K - Regulation FD >INR
data.description:
Infinity Natural Resources Inc. (INR) filed a Form 8K - Regulation FD Disclosure - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on April 14, 2026.

On April 13, 2026, the Company issued a press release announcing Mr. McNeill's appointment to the Board. A copy of the press release is furnished as Exhibit 99.1 hereto and incorporated herein by reference.

The information furnished in this Current Report on Form 8-K pursuant to Item 7.01, including Exhibit 99.1, shall not be deemed "filed" for any purpose, including for purposes of Section 18 of the Exchange Act, or otherwise be subject to the liabilities of that Section, nor shall it be deemed to be incorporated by reference in any filing of the Company under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, or the Exchange Act, except as expressly set forth by specific reference in such filing.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/2029118/000202911826000023/inr-20260413.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/2029118/000202911826000023/0002029118-26-000023-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: Infinity Natural Rscs Files 8K - Director, Officer or Compensation Filing >INR
data.description:
Infinity Natural Resources Inc. (INR) filed a Form 8K - Director, Officer or Compensation Filing - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on April 14, 2026.

On April 13, 2026, the Board of Directors (the "Board") of Infinity Natural Resources, Inc. (the "Company") appointed Scott McNeill to the Board, effective immediately, to fill a current vacancy on the Board and to serve for an initial term expiring at the 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders of the Company.

Mr. McNeill is currently the Chief Executive Officer of Peak Rentals, LLC, an equipment rental company serving the energy industry. He is also the Managing Member of Headwall Capital, an investment company pursuing opportunities in the energy sector. From 2021 to 2024, Mr. McNeill held multiple senior leadership roles at Black Mountain Sand Holdings LLC ("BMS"), including Executive Chairman, Chief Executive Officer, and Chief Financial Officer, and served on its board of directors. His leadership at BMS culminated in its merger with Covia Energy to form Iron Oak Energy Solutions LLC ("Iron Oak"), and he currently serves on Iron Oak's board of directors. Prior to joining Iron Oak, from 2019 to 2021, he was co-founder and Chief Executive Officer of Switchback Energy Acquisition Corporation (NYSE: SBE) and Co-Chief Executive Officer of Switchback II Corporation (NYSE: SWBK), serving on the boards of both entities. From 2013 until its merger in 2018 with Concho Resources Inc. (NYSE: CXO), Mr. McNeill served as Chief Financial Officer and a director of RSP Permian Inc. (NYSE: RSPP), an upstream oil and gas company. Before joining RSP Permian, Mr. McNeill spent 15 years in energy investment banking and most recently served as a managing director in the energy investment banking group of Raymond James Financial, Inc., advising companies operating in the exploration and production, midstream, and energy service and equipment segments of the energy industry. Mr. McNeill holds a B.B.A from Baylor University and an M.B.A from the University of Texas at Austin and is a certified public accountant in the State of Texas. Mr. McNeill brings extensive experience in energy, finance, accounting, and capital markets, including as a public company executive as CEO and CFO, as well as prior public board service, to the Board.

Mr. McNeill was also appointed to the Audit Committee of the Board, effective immediately. Mr. McNeill will receive the standard compensation paid by the Company to its non-employee directors, as described under "Director Compensation" in the Company's Proxy Statement for its 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders, which was filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on April 14, 2026. Additionally, the Company purchases and maintains directors' and officers' liability insurance for, and provides indemnification to, each member of the Board. In connection with this appointment, the Company and Mr. McNeill entered into the Company's standard indemnification agreement for directors, the form of which has been filed with the Company's Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2025.

The Board affirmatively determined that Mr. McNeill is an independent director within the meaning of the New York Stock Exchange listing standards and Rule 10A-3 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "Exchange Act"). There is no arrangement or understanding between Mr. McNeill and any other person pursuant to which he was selected as a director. There are no family relationships between Mr. McNeill and any other Board member or executive officer. Mr. McNeill is not a party to any transaction with any related person required to be disclosed pursuant to Item 404(a) of Regulation S-K.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/2029118/000202911826000023/inr-20260413.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/2029118/000202911826000023/0002029118-26-000023-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.
